assignment.name: vague
assignment.title: Make something cool with AI
assignment.description: Build a project that uses AI in some interesting way. The exact scope, audience, and form of the deliverable are up to you.

advisory.reason: consultant budget exhausted; some leaves were not evaluated

verdict.notes: This plan mistakes tool availability for project scope. Running Stable Diffusion locally is a 2-hour setup task, not a 14-day project. Without a clear problem, audience, or evaluation framework, you're building a demo, not demonstrating systems thinking. Either anchor on a specific domain where image generation solves a real problem (e.g., architectural iteration loops, design feedback cycles) or commit to measuring emergent behavior—as written, this is a tutorial completion, not a project.

Use a pre-built API (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion API)

verdict.notes: This plan mistakes 'interesting' for 'easy.' Wrapping a pre-built API with minimal domain logic is not a systems thinking project—it's glue code. With 14 days, you should be modeling *how* something works (a causal system, agent dynamics, or feedback loops), not just piping data through someone else's black box. If you want to use generative AI, build a system that *reasons* about its outputs or explores emergent behavior under constraints.

verdict.notes: This is a list of application categories, not a project plan. There is no specified model, no clear scope boundary, no defined deliverable, and no demonstrated systems thinking. Anand expects a causal model (CLD or ABM) with a specific domain and measurable emergent behavior—not a tooling exercise. Even with 14 days, this plan lacks the rigor to be evaluated for feasibility.

verdict.notes: This is a list of possible project types, not a plan. There is no declared model, no system to understand, no scenario comparison, and no clear deliverable. Anand requires you to pick one specific thing to build and explain *why* it's interesting as a systems problem—not just that it uses AI. A chatbot wrapper around an LLM API is not a modeling project; it's a demo. If you want to model something (e.g., how conversational AI shapes team communication dynamics, or how a content generation pipeline's failure modes propagate), say so explicitly and scope the model itself.

verdict.notes: This is a list of three unrelated product categories, not a project plan. There is no specific problem, no chosen AI approach, no model or system design, and no clear deliverable. Anand will ask: Which one are you building? What is the actual technical architecture? How will you evaluate whether it works? Without answers, there is nothing to grade.
